status: DRAFT validation/reconciliation gate shapes for future bi-sql-knowledge (Slice 5). Not executable; reasoning templates only.

patterns.intent: Assert a key is unique (no duplicate rows per key).
patterns.sql_shape: SELECT key, COUNT(*) FROM t GROUP BY key HAVING COUNT(*) > 1

patterns.intent: Assert required columns are not null.
patterns.sql_shape: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM t WHERE required_col IS NULL

patterns.intent: Assert every fact foreign key resolves to a dimension row (no orphans).
patterns.sql_shape: SELECT f.* FROM fact f LEFT JOIN dim d ON d.key=f.key WHERE d.key IS NULL

patterns.intent: Assert row count matches an expected value or the prior layer.
patterns.sql_shape: SELECT (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM a) AS a_rows, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM b) AS b_rows

patterns.intent: Assert a value control total is invariant across layers.
patterns.sql_shape: WITH s AS(SELECT SUM(expr) v FROM source), t AS(SELECT SUM(expr) v FROM target) SELECT s.v-t.v AS diff FROM s CROSS JOIN t

patterns.intent: Assert the latest data is within the freshness SLA.
patterns.sql_shape: SELECT CURRENT_DATE - MAX(date_column) AS days_stale FROM t

patterns.intent: Assert every expected period is present (no missing days/months).
patterns.sql_shape: SELECT d.date FROM date d LEFT JOIN fact f ON f.date=d.date WHERE d.date IN window GROUP BY d.date HAVING COUNT(f.key)=0

patterns.intent: Assert one row per key after a dedup step (and idempotency).
patterns.sql_shape: SELECT COUNT(*) AS rows, COUNT(DISTINCT key) AS keys FROM t

patterns.intent: Assert values fall within an expected domain/range (or allowed set).
patterns.sql_shape: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM t WHERE column < min OR column > max  -- or column NOT IN (allowed set)

patterns.intent: Prove two tables hold identical rows (row-level reconciliation).
patterns.pass_condition: A EXCEPT B = 0 rows AND B EXCEPT A = 0 rows AND COUNT(*) equal
patterns.sql_shape: (SELECT cols FROM a EXCEPT SELECT cols FROM b) and (SELECT cols FROM b EXCEPT SELECT cols FROM a) returns nothing; counts equal

patterns.intent: Metadata-driven standard column profile across many tables/columns.
patterns.pass_condition: profiles within expected bounds / no unexpected drift vs prior run
patterns.sql_shape: From information_schema.columns, generate per-column: rows, nulls, distinct, min/max; compare to expectations/prior
